name: Job Purnell's wife and child in the 1851 Trowbridge census
researcher_question: Who was the wife of Job Purnell, and who was their child, as recorded in the 1851 Census of Trowbridge, Wiltshire, England?
notes: Subject is Job Purnell (LHKV-VKD), b. ~1826 Trowbridge, a Wiltshire cloth-town laborer who stayed in England (NOT the same-named emigrant 2W2F-CNH, who was in Wisconsin by 1851). Wife (Serena/Serinah Deacon), the couple relationship + 1849 marriage, and son Samuel are all stripped; the agent must recover the wife and child from the 1851 census household itself. The answer co-resides with the subject in the named record, so identity and answer are recovered together — this is why the wife/child question is solvable where a parents question on the same crowded name is not. Watch for surname mis-indexing (Purnell/Purnall) and other Job Purnells in Trowbridge; the correct subject is the b.~1826 laborer with an infant son in 1851.
